$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `conversation_chunks` repo (Phase-2).
One committed file drives BOTH sides of the differential:
- the v4 oracle (build-conversation-chunks-fixture.ts +
conversation-chunks-tier2.ts) and
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness conversation_chunks tier-2 test).
BLOB column, after `help_docs`: `embedding` stores a raw Float32 byte buffer
(little-endian f32 bytes). v4's `documentToRow` converts a
number[]/Float32Array to a BLOB via `embeddingToBlob`; an EMPTY array or null
is stored as NULL. The canonical dump emits BLOBs as lowercase hex on BOTH
sides, so deterministic embeddings compare bit-exact.
EMBEDDING VALUES are chosen to be EXACTLY float32-representable so the
f64->f32 cast is lossless and identical on both sides. [0.5,-0.25,0.75,0.125]
encodes little-endian as `0000003f000080be0000403f0000003e`.
interchangeIndex is `z.number().int().min(0)` (min only, no max) -> REAL.
Integer-valued cells dump back as JSON integers (0, 1, 2).
participantNames / messageIds are JSON array columns -> compact JSON text.
BANKED BEHAVIOR: a text-only-ish `update` (content + interchangeIndex +
participantNames) on a row that HAS an embedding must LEAVE the embedding
BLOB untouched. v4's `_update` rewrites the whole row from the hydrated
existing entity (the BLOB round-trips losslessly); the Rust partial
`UPDATE SET` simply never names the embedding column. The post-update dump
must still show the original embedding hex — op #3 below exercises this.
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one the
folders / tags / help-docs fixtures use. It is NOT the real Friday pepper
and is safe to commit.
DETERMINISM: every id and timestamp is pinned, so both sides produce
byte-identical post-op state and the canonical dump needs ZERO normalization.

seed.content: Aurora greets the salon at dawn.

ops.comment: New chunk WITH an embedding + populated arrays. Exercises the BLOB insert path (number[] -> little-endian Float32 bytes -> hex `0000003f000080be0000403f0000003e`) and the JSON array columns. interchangeIndex 2. id + timestamps pinned.
New chunk with NULL embedding + EMPTY arrays. Exercises the empty/null -> SQL NULL path and the empty JSON array `[]`. interchangeIndex 3.

Update on the EMBEDDED seed row `Aurora`: change content + interchangeIndex + participantNames only (NO embedding field). The embedding BLOB must SURVIVE untouched — the post-update dump still shows `0000003f000080be0000403f0000003e`. createdAt preserved, updatedAt pinned.

data.content: Aurora greets the salon at dawn, rewritten.

ops.comment: TEXT-ONLY update on the NULL-embedding seed row `Prospero`: change content + messageIds. Embedding stays NULL. createdAt preserved, updatedAt pinned.

data.content: Prospero replies with a grander flourish.

ops.comment: Delete the NULL-embedding `Carina` chunk created above.
